summary: EXP-201 addresses WIN-012.c5 H2 (major): the review was attacking a dome position the author has moved past. Adds ONE new paragraph (381 words) to WIN-012 detail_evidence, inserted after the existing 'WIN-011 concession is fatal to κ' paragraph and before the 'derivation is also circular' paragraph. The new paragraph names the dome's current live-site mechanism ('dielectric boundary transition model' with verbatim quote about onset/termination of firmament-plate shading driving the induction pulse at C1/C4), credits the kernel of truth (the dome has moved empirically in the right direction by matching Yang & Wang 2002's C1/C4 valleys), and then lands three problems fatal to κ: (i) magnitude mismatch — live PRED-TIER-2 −10 to −13 µGal is 50-100% larger than the −6.5 µGal Mohe datum that anchors κ; (ii) self-filtering detector — the dome's pipeline rescue for the 1999/2009 SG nulls suppresses exactly the C1/C4 signal the new mechanism predicts; (iii) parameter adoption, not prediction — κ retained from the abandoned shielding interpretation without re-derivation under the new mechanism. Verbatim dome quotes independently re-verified via WebFetch this run. patch_type is paragraph_addition with explicit patch_instructions specifying insertion point. No TLDR change recommended (existing TLDR still captures the Self-Contradicted verdict correctly). IMPORTANT SIDE-NOTE: c5 H1 (critical) — fabricated 'E-PRED-C' prediction label still present in current detail_evidence — is OUT OF SCOPE for EXP-201 but UNRESOLVED. Decider should either route H1 to a new expansion or file a companion issue; it is the highest-severity c5 finding and the line remains in the live text. See EXP-201 unaddressed_holes_note for details.
